steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The wife says she doesn't find art compelling. Schmidt responds with physical reactions—wincing, sighing dramatically, and leaving with a fabricated excuse—rather than directly stating his opinion.

steps.title: Match the behavior to the answer
steps.content: D correctly recognizes that Schmidt conveys his feelings without explicitly stating them—he communicates disapproval entirely through his actions and reactions. The other choices either make unsupported claims about his behavior (A: that he only acts theatrically with this couple; B: that he's absentminded) or invent details not present in the text (C: a previous disagreement). That matches choice D — D is correct.
